05-28.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Nicetas the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

05-29.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Tyre
05-29.text: Thy lamb Virgin-martyr Theodosia of Tyre, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

05-30.title: Troparion of St. Isaac of Dalmatia
05-30.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Isaac of Dalmatia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

05-31.title: Troparion of Apostle Hermas of the Seventy
05-31.text: O Holy Apostle Apostle Hermas of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

06-01.title: Troparion of the Martyr Justin the Philosopher
06-01.text: Receiving godly wisdom through love of Christ and the radiance of the Holy Spirit, thou didst illumine the souls of the faithful with thy words and didst shame the demons of heathen sophistry. Pray to Christ God for our souls, O philosopher Justin.

06-02.title: Troparion of St. Nicephorus the Confessor
06-02.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Nicephorus the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-03.title: Troparion of Martyr Lucillian and those with him
06-03.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Lucillian and those with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-04.title: Troparion of St. Metrophanes
06-04.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Metrophanes, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-05.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Dorotheus
06-05.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Dorotheus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-06.title: Troparion of St. Hilarion the New of Dalmatia
06-06.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Hilarion the New of Dalmatia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-07.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Theodotus of Ancyra
06-07.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Theodotus of Ancyra, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-08.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro (Stratelates)
06-08.text: Thy Great-martyr Translation of the Relics of Great-martyr Theodore the Tyro (Stratelates), O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-09.title: Troparion of St. Cyril
06-09.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Cyril, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-10.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Timothy
06-10.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Timothy, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-11.title: Troparion of Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas
06-11.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostles Bartholomew and Barnabas, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

06-12.title: Troparion of St. Onuphrius the Great
06-12.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Onuphrius the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-13.title: Troparion of Martyr Aquilina of Byblos
06-13.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Aquilina of Byblos, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-14.title: Troparion of the Prophet Elisha
06-14.text: O great Prophet Elisha, thou didst receive a double portion of the spirit of Elijah, and the gift of healing all sicknesses and infirmities of body and soul. Pray now to the Lord that mercy may be granted to our souls.

06-15.title: Troparion of Prophet Amos
06-15.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Prophet Amos, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

06-16.title: Troparion of St. Tikhon the Wonderworker
06-16.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Tikhon the Wonderworker, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-17.title: Troparion of Martyrs Manuel
06-17.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Manuel, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Leontius
06-18.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Leontius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-19.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Jude
06-19.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostle Jude, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

06-20.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Methodius
06-20.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Methodius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-21.title: Troparion of Martyr Julian of Tarsus
06-21.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Julian of Tarsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-22.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Eusebius
06-22.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Eusebius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

06-23.title: Troparion of Martyr Agrippina of Rome
06-23.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Agrippina of Rome, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

06-24.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Forerunner
06-24.text: O Prophet and Forerunner of the coming of Christ, though we honor thee with love, we cannot worthily extol thee, for thy mother's barrenness and thy father's voicelessness were undone by thy honored and glorious nativity, and the Incarnation of the Son of God was proclaimed to the world.

06-25.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Febronia of Nisibis
06-25.text: Thy lamb Virgin-martyr Febronia of Nisibis, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

06-26.title: Troparion of St. David of Thessalonica
06-26.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. David of Thessalonica, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-27.title: Troparion of St. Sampson the Hospitable
06-27.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Sampson the Hospitable, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

06-28.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of the Unmercenaries Cyrus and John
06-28.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Translation of the Relics of the Unmercenaries Cyrus and John, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

06-29.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
06-29.text: O first-throned of the Apostles and teachers of the world, intercede with the Master of all that He may grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

06-29.title: Kontakion of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
06-29.text: O Lord, Thou hast received the steadfast and divinely-inspired heralds, the chief among Thine Apostles, into the enjoyment of Thy good things and into Thy rest, for Thou hast accepted their labors and their death as above every burnt offering, O Thou who alone knowest the hearts of men.

06-30.title: Troparion of Synaxis of the Holy
06-30.text: O Holy Apostle Synaxis of the Holy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-01.title: Troparion of Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Rome
07-01.text: O holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Rome, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

07-02.title: Troparion of St. John of Shanghai and San Francisco
07-02.text: Lo, thy care for thy flock in its sojourn hath prefigured the supplications which thou dost ever offer up for the whole world. Thus do we believe, having come to know thy love, O Holy Hierarch John the Wonderworker. Wholly sanctified by God through the ministry of the all-pure Mysteries, and thyself ever strengthened thereby, thou didst hasten to the suffering, O most gladsome healer. Hasten now also to the aid of us who honor thee with all our heart.

07-03.title: Troparion of Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea in Cappadocia
07-03.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea in Cappadocia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-04.title: Troparion of St. Andrew of Crete
07-04.text: By thy holy life thou didst shine forth as a star in the firmament of the Church, O Father Andrew; thou didst pour forth like a fountain the sweetness of psalmody, and didst nourish the faithful through hymns of penitence. Pray to Christ God to save our souls.

07-05.title: Troparion of St. Athanasius of Mount Athos
07-05.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Athanasius of Mount Athos, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

07-06.title: Troparion of St. Sisoes the Great
07-06.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Sisoes the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

07-07.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Kyriake of Nicomedia
07-07.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr Kyriake of Nicomedia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-08.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Procopius of Caesarea in Palestine
07-08.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr Procopius of Caesarea in Palestine, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-09.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Pancratius
07-09.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Pancratius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

07-10.title: Troparion of the Venerable Anthony of the Caves
07-10.text: Having departed from the tumults of the world, with faith thou didst leave thy fatherland, and didst follow Christ, settling in the holy mountain of Athos; and thence, by the command of the Mother of God, thou didst come to the mountains of Kiev. There thou didst end thy life in fastings, fathering many monks who came to thee. Wherefore, having received from God the gift of wonders, O Venerable Anthony, pray for our souls.

07-11.title: Troparion of St. Olga of Kiev, Equal-to-the-Apostles
07-11.text: Giving wings of mind to the knowledge of God, thou hast spread thy great desires to look upon Christ. Thou hast also led thy beloved grandson Vladimir along the path which thou hast trod. Wherefore, O Olga, equal-to-the-apostles, we celebrate thy honored memory before Christ God, asking forgiveness of our sins.

07-12.title: Troparion of the Venerable Paisios the Athonite
07-12.text: The dweller of Mount Athos and the boast of Cappadocia, the new gift of God to the faithful — let us honor him with hymns, O lovers of the saints; for the Paraclete enriched him with the multitude of His gifts, and thou hast become a shelter for all who hasten to thee, O blessed Paisios, our Holy Father.

07-13.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
07-13.text: Captains of the heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy beseech thee that by your supplications thou wilt encompass us beneath the wings of thy immaterial glory, and faithfully preserve us who fall down and cry to thee: deliver us from all dangers, for ye are the commanders of the powers on high.

07-14.title: Troparion of Apostle Aquila of the Seventy
07-14.text: O Holy Apostle Apostle Aquila of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-15.title: Troparion of St. Vladimir of Kiev, Equal-to-the-Apostles
07-15.text: Thou wast like a merchant seeking a goodly pearl, O sovereign Vladimir, sitting upon the high throne of the mother of the cities of God, the God-saved Kiev. For having tested and sent forth thine envoys to the Imperial City to behold the Orthodox Faith, thou didst find the priceless pearl, even Christ, who from eternity chose thee as another Paul, and shook off all blindness, both spiritual and bodily, in the holy font. Therefore, we thy children celebrate thy repose: entreat Christ to save the Russian land and to grant our souls great mercy.

07-16.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Athenogenes
07-16.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Athenogenes, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

07-17.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Marina
07-17.text: Thy lamb Marina, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking thee I contest. I am crucified with thee, and in thy baptism am buried with thee. I suffer for thy sake, that I might reign with thee; I die for thee, that I might live in thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to thee in love. By her prayers save our souls, O thou who art merciful.

07-17.title: Troparion of the Royal Martyrs of Russia
07-17.text: Most-noble and right-believing passion-bearers, gentle Tsar Nicholas and his most-pious consort Alexandra, with their God-beloved children Olga, Tatiana, Maria, Anastasia, and the Tsarevich Alexei: ye were martyred for Christ by the godless, the icons of every Russian Christian household to come. Pray to the merciful Master for our country, that we may walk in His paths and find rest with you in His mercy.

07-18.title: Troparion of the New Martyr Grand Duchess Elizabeth
07-18.text: Emulating the Lord's self-abasement on the earth, O Grand Duchess Elizabeth, thou didst give up the comforts of palaces to dwell in the slums, console the suffering, and labor as a sister of mercy among the poor; entering thus into the joy of the bridegroom Christ, with the sister Varvara thou wast crowned with martyrdom by the godless. With them pray to the lover of mankind, that He save our souls.

07-19.title: Troparion of St. Macrina
07-19.text: In thee, O Mother, was carefully preserved what was according to the image; for taking up thy cross, thou didst follow Christ. By so doing, thou didst teach us to disregard the flesh, for it passeth away, but to care for the soul as a thing immortal. Wherefore, O righteous Macrina, thy spirit rejoiceth with the angels.

07-20.title: Troparion of the Prophet Elijah
07-20.text: O angelic incarnate prophet, foundation of prophets, the second forerunner of the coming of Christ, glorious Elijah, who from on high didst send down grace upon Elisha to dispel diseases and cleanse lepers, for those who honor thee thou pourest forth abundant healings.

07-21.title: Troparion of Sts. Symeon the Fool-for-Christ and John
07-21.text: O holy Sts. Symeon the Fool-for-Christ and John, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

07-22.title: Troparion of St. Mary Magdalene
07-22.text: Christ, who was born of the Virgin for our sake, did Thou deliver from Whom didst expel seven demons, holy Mary Magdalene. Thou didst become the first to behold the divine Resurrection, and as the equal-to-the-Apostles, thou didst preach Him risen from the dead unto all the ends of the earth.

07-23.title: Troparion of Martyrs Trophimus
07-23.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Trophimus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-24.title: Troparion of the Holy Passion-Bearers Boris and Gleb
07-24.text: True passion-bearers and observers of the gospel commandments, blessed Boris and Gleb, you did not resist the attacks of your brother, your enemy; he killed your bodies, yet was unable to touch your souls. Therefore let evil rulers tremble, while the choirs of angels rejoice, praising the pair who pleased God. Together, intercede that our country may be saved and our souls find great mercy.

07-25.title: Troparion of the Dormition of Saint Anna
07-25.text: O Foremother of Christ, we entreat thee to send down the favors of thy daughter, the Theotokos, upon those who hymn thy memory in faith. Thou hast brought into the world the holy Mother of God who bore the eternal life. Wherefore, with joy in thy dormition thou hast departed to the Lord. Pray for those who keep thy memory.

07-26.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Hermolaus and those with him
07-26.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Hermolaus and those with him, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

07-27.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Panteleimon
07-27.text: O holy prize-winner and healer Panteleimon, intercede with our merciful God to grant our souls forgiveness of sins.

07-28.title: Troparion of Holy Apostles and Deacons Prochorus
07-28.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostles and Deacons Prochorus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-29.title: Troparion of Martyr Callinicus of Cilicia
07-29.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Callinicus of Cilicia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

07-30.title: Troparion of Apostles Silas
07-30.text: O Holy Apostle Apostles Silas, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

07-31.title: Troparion of Righteous Eudokimos of Cappadocia
07-31.text: Great are the achievements of faith! O Righteous Righteous Eudokimos of Cappadocia, thou didst shine like a star upon the earth, leaving us a model of holy life. Through thy prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

08-01.title: Troparion of the Honorable Cross
08-01.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance, granting unto Thy people victory over all their enemies, and by the power of Thy Cross preserving Thy commonwealth.

08-02.title: Troparion of Translation of the Relics of the Protomartyr and Archdeacon Stephen
08-02.text: Thy Martyr Translation of the Relics of the Protomartyr and Archdeacon Stephen, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-03.title: Troparion of St. Anthony the Roman
08-03.text: O Wonderworker St. Anthony the Roman, thou wast filled with grace from on high and didst pour out unto the faithful the gift of healing. Pray Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

08-04.title: Troparion of The Seven Holy Youths of Ephesus
08-04.text: O Holy Apostle The Seven Holy Youths of Ephesus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-05.title: Troparion of Forefeast of the Holy Transfiguration; Martyr Eusignius
08-05.text: Thy Martyr Forefeast of the Holy Transfiguration; Martyr Eusignius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-06.title: Troparion of the Transfiguration
08-06.text: Thou wast transfigured on the mount, O Christ God, revealing Thy glory to Thy disciples as far as they could bear it. Let Thine everlasting light shine also upon us sinners, through the prayers of the Theotokos. O Giver of light, glory to Thee.

08-06.title: Kontakion of the Transfiguration
08-06.text: On the mountain wast Thou transfigured, O Christ God, and Thy disciples beheld Thy glory as far as they could bear it, so that when they would behold Thee crucified they would understand Thy voluntary Passion, and proclaim to the world that Thou art truly the radiance of the Father.

08-07.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Dometius the Persian
08-07.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Dometius the Persian, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

08-08.title: Troparion of St. Emilian the Confessor
08-08.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Emilian the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

08-09.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Matthias
08-09.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostle Matthias, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-10.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr and Archdeacon Lawrence of Rome
08-10.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr and Archdeacon Lawrence of Rome, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

08-11.title: Troparion of Martyr Euplus the Deacon of Catania
08-11.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Euplus the Deacon of Catania, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-12.title: Troparion of Martyrs Photius and Anicetus of Nicomedia
08-12.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Photius and Anicetus of Nicomedia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-13.title: Troparion of St. Tikhon
08-13.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Tikhon, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

08-14.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
08-14.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

08-15.title: Troparion of the Dormition
08-15.text: In giving birth thou didst preserve thy virginity; in thy dormition thou didst not forsake the world, O Theotokos. Thou wast translated unto life, since thou art the Mother of Life, and by thine intercessions thou dost deliver our souls from death.

08-15.title: Kontakion of the Dormition
08-15.text: Neither the tomb nor death had power over the Theotokos, who is sleepless in her intercessions and unfailing hope in her protection; for as the Mother of Life she hath been translated unto life by Him who dwelt in her ever-virgin womb.

08-16.title: Troparion of the Feast
08-16.text: Translation of the Image of Christ Not-Made-by-Hands from Edessa — let us celebrate the great mystery of our salvation, the kindness of God in His coming, the love of God for the world.

08-17.title: Troparion of Martyr Myron of Cyzicus
08-17.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Myron of Cyzicus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-18.title: Troparion of Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria
08-18.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-19.title: Troparion of Martyr Andrew Stratelates and 2593 with him
08-19.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Andrew Stratelates and 2593 with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-20.title: Troparion of the Prophet Samuel
08-20.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Samuel, O Lord: through him we beseech thee, save our souls.

08-21.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Thaddaeus of the Seventy
08-21.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostle Thaddaeus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-22.title: Troparion of Martyr Agathonicus of Nicomedia
08-22.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Agathonicus of Nicomedia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-23.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
08-23.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

08-24.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Eutyches
08-24.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Eutyches, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

08-25.title: Troparion of Return of the Relics of the Apostle Bartholomew; Apostle Titus of the Seventy
08-25.text: O Holy Apostle Return of the Relics of the Apostle Bartholomew; Apostle Titus of the Seventy, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

08-26.title: Troparion of Martyrs Adrian and Natalia
08-26.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Adrian and Natalia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

08-27.title: Troparion of St. Poemen the Great
08-27.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Poemen the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

08-28.title: Troparion of the Venerable Moses the Black
08-28.text: Thou didst forsake Egypt's wickedness and didst draw nigh unto God, O Venerable Father Moses; thou didst earnestly take up the cross of asceticism, and didst become a wonderworker, the shepherd of lions and of the souls who came to thee. Pray for those who keep thy memory, that Christ may grant us His great mercy.

08-29.title: Troparion of the Beheading of the Forerunner
08-29.text: The just man is remembered with songs of praise, but for thee the testimony of the Lord is sufficient, O Forerunner. Truly thou wast revealed more honorable than the prophets, for thou wast deemed worthy to baptize in the Jordan Him whom they had preached. Therefore having contested for the truth, thou didst rejoice to bring the good tidings even to those in hell — that God hath appeared in the flesh, taking away the sin of the world and granting us great mercy.

08-30.title: Troparion of Sts. Alexander
08-30.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Sts. Alexander, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

08-31.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
08-31.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

09-01.title: Troparion of St. Symeon the Stylite
09-01.text: Thou didst prove to be a pillar of patience and didst emulate the Forefathers, O righteous one: Job in his sufferings, Joseph in his temptations, the angelic life among them in the flesh. O Symeon, our righteous Father, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

09-02.title: Troparion of Martyr Mamas of Caesarea
09-02.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Mamas of Caesarea, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-03.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Anthimus
09-03.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Anthimus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-04.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Babylas of Antioch; Prophet Moses the God-Seer
09-04.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Babylas of Antioch; Prophet Moses the God-Seer, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-05.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Zechariah
09-05.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Zechariah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

09-06.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
09-06.text: Captains of the heavenly hosts, we who are unworthy beseech thee that by your supplications thou wilt encompass us beneath the wings of thy immaterial glory, and faithfully preserve us who fall down and cry to thee: deliver us from all dangers, for ye are the commanders of the powers on high.

09-07.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
09-07.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

09-08.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
09-08.text: Thy nativity, O Theotokos, has proclaimed joy to the whole universe: for from thee did shine forth the Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God. He hath loosed the curse and granted the blessing; He hath overcome death and bestowed on us eternal life.

09-08.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
09-08.text: By thy nativity, O most pure Virgin, Joachim and Anna were freed from the reproach of barrenness, and Adam and Eve from the corruption of death. Thy people, redeemed from the guilt of their sins, exult and cry to thee: 'The barren one bringeth forth the Theotokos and the nourisher of our Life.'

09-09.title: Troparion of the Ancestors of God Joachim and Anna
09-09.text: O Joachim and Anna, righteous ancestors of God, ye have received from God the freedom that ye sought through long childlessness — for from your loins was sprung the Virgin who bore the Lord. Pray with her, that He grant us great mercy.

09-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Menodora
09-10.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Menodora, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-11.title: Troparion of St. Theodora of Alexandria
09-11.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Theodora of Alexandria, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

09-12.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Autonomus
09-12.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Autonomus, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

09-13.title: Troparion of Commemoration of the Dedication of the Church of the Resurrection at Jerusalem
09-13.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father Commemoration of the Dedication of the Church of the Resurrection at Jerusalem, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

09-14.title: Troparion of the Exaltation of the Cross
09-14.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance. Grant victory to the Orthodox Christians over their adversaries, and by the power of Thy Cross, preserve Thy commonwealth.

09-14.title: Kontakion of the Exaltation of the Cross
09-14.text: O Christ our God, Thou wast lifted up upon the Cross of Thine own will. Therefore grant Thy mercies unto Thy new commonwealth which is called by Thy name. By Thy power gladden the Orthodox Christians, and grant them victory over every adversary, having as an ally that weapon of peace, the trophy invincible.

09-15.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Nicetas the Goth
09-15.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr Nicetas the Goth, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-16.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Euphemia the All-praised
09-16.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr Euphemia the All-praised, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-17.title: Troparion of Sophia and her Daughters Faith, Hope, and Love
09-17.text: The Church doth celebrate and praise as three sweet-scented flowers Faith, Hope and Love, divinely thriving; and crieth out in song to their mother Sophia, who is named for divine wisdom: Through the contests of these thy children entreat the only Lover of mankind that we be granted mercy and the divine vision.

09-18.title: Troparion of St. Eumenius the Wonderworker
09-18.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Eumenius the Wonderworker, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

09-19.title: Troparion of Martyrs Trophimus
09-19.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Trophimus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-20.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Eustathius Placidas
09-20.text: Thy holy martyr Eustathius, O Lord, through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from Thee, our God. For having Thy strength, he laid low his adversaries, and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. Through his intercessions, save our souls.

09-21.title: Troparion of the Honorable Cross
09-21.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance, granting unto Thy people victory over all their enemies, and by the power of Thy Cross preserving Thy commonwealth.

09-22.title: Troparion of the Prophet Jonah
09-22.text: As thou didst foretell mystically the three-day burial of Christ, and didst preach repentance unto Nineveh, do thou ever pray for us, O Prophet Jonah.

09-23.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
09-23.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

09-24.title: Troparion of the Protomartyr Thekla
09-24.text: O Equal-to-the-Apostles first-suffering virgin Thekla, by the teachings of Paul thou didst put on the armor of Christ, and didst master the imaginings of evil; thou didst overthrow the tortures of the impious, and by the sign of the Cross thou didst turn aside the violence of nature. Pray for us, that He may grant our souls great mercy.

09-25.title: Troparion of St. Sergius of Radonezh
09-25.text: Champion of virtues, true warrior of Christ our God: thou didst struggle greatly against the passions in this transient life, in psalmody, vigil, and fasting, becoming an example to thy disciples. Therefore the All-Holy Spirit dwelt in thee, and by His action thou wast adorned with splendor; and since thou hast great boldness before the Holy Trinity, remember the flock thou hast gathered, wise one, and as thou didst promise, do not forget to visit thy children, O venerable Sergius, our Father.

09-26.title: Troparion of Repose of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian
09-26.text: O Holy Apostle Repose of the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

09-27.title: Troparion of Martyr Callistratus and the forty-nine soldiers with him
09-27.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Callistratus and the forty-nine soldiers with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

09-28.title: Troparion of St. Chariton the Confessor
09-28.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Chariton the Confessor, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

09-29.title: Troparion of St. Cyriacus the Hermit
09-29.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Cyriacus the Hermit, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

09-30.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Gregory the Illuminator of Armenia
09-30.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Gregory the Illuminator of Armenia, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-01.title: Troparion of St. Romanos the Melodist
10-01.text: In thee, O Father, was carefully preserved what is according to the image; for, having taken up thy cross, thou didst follow Christ; by thy deeds thou hast taught us to despise the flesh, for it passeth away, but to attend to the soul as a thing immortal: wherefore, O venerable Romanos, thy spirit rejoiceth with the angels.

10-02.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Cyprian and Martyr Justina
10-02.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Cyprian and Martyr Justina, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-03.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Dionysius the Areopagite
10-03.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Dionysius the Areopagite, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-04.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Hierotheus of Athens
10-04.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Hierotheus of Athens, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-05.title: Troparion of Martyr Charitina of Amisus
10-05.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Charitina of Amisus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-06.title: Troparion of the Apostle Thomas
10-06.text: Holy Apostle Thomas, intercede with the merciful God that He grant unto our souls forgiveness of sins.

10-07.title: Troparion of Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus
10-07.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-08.title: Troparion of St. Pelagia the Penitent
10-08.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Pelagia the Penitent, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

10-09.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle James
10-09.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostle James, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia
10-10.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-11.title: Troparion of Holy Apostle Philip
10-11.text: O Holy Apostle Holy Apostle Philip, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-12.title: Troparion of Martyrs Probus
10-12.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Probus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-13.title: Troparion of Martyrs Carpus
10-13.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Carpus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-14.title: Troparion of Martyrs Nazarius
10-14.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Nazarius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-15.title: Troparion of St. Euthymius the New of Thessalonica
10-15.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Euthymius the New of Thessalonica, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

10-16.title: Troparion of Martyr Longinus the Centurion
10-16.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Longinus the Centurion, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-17.title: Troparion of Lazarus the Four-Days-Dead
10-17.text: Giving us before thy Passion an assurance of the general resurrection, thou didst raise Lazarus from the dead, O Christ our God. Wherefore, like the children of old, we also bear before thee the symbols of triumph and cry to thee, the Vanquisher of death: Hosanna in the highest; blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

10-18.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist Luke
10-18.text: O holy Apostle and Evangelist Luke, intercede with the merciful God that He grant unto our souls forgiveness of sins.

10-19.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Joel
10-19.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Joel, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

10-20.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Artemius of Antioch
10-20.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr Artemius of Antioch, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-21.title: Troparion of St. Hilarion the Great
10-21.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Hilarion the Great, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

10-22.title: Troparion of Equal-to-the-Apostles Abercius
10-22.text: O Holy Apostle Equal-to-the-Apostles Abercius, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

10-23.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle James, Brother of the Lord
10-23.text: As the Lord's disciple thou didst receive the Gospel, O righteous one; as a martyr thou hast that which cannot be despised; thou hast boldness as the Brother of God; as a hierarch thou hast the gift of intercession. Pray to Christ God that our souls may be saved.

10-24.title: Troparion of Martyr Arethas and his companions of Najran
10-24.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Arethas and his companions of Najran, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-25.title: Troparion of Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius the Notaries
10-25.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius the Notaries, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-26.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Demetrius of Thessalonica
10-26.text: The world has found in thee a great champion in time of trouble, as thou didst defeat the heathen, O victorious one. As thou didst stop the boasting of Lyaeus, and inspire Nestor to courage in combat, so, O holy great-martyr Demetrius, entreat Christ God to grant us great mercy.

10-26.title: Kontakion of St. Demetrius
10-26.text: God, who gave thee invincible strength and with care kept thy city unharmed, royally clothed the Church in the purple of thy blood, for thou art her strength, O Demetrius.

10-27.title: Troparion of Martyr Nestor of Thessalonica
10-27.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Nestor of Thessalonica, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-28.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Paraskeva
10-28.text: O Paraskeva, athlete worthy of all praise, thou didst contend in suffering, and the Bridegroom Christ adorned thee with the crown of incorruption. Therefore intercede with Him that our souls may be saved.

10-29.title: Troparion of Virgin-martyr Anastasia the Roman
10-29.text: Thy lamb Virgin-martyr Anastasia the Roman, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

10-30.title: Troparion of Martyr Zenobius and his sister Zenobia
10-30.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Zenobius and his sister Zenobia, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

10-31.title: Troparion of Apostles Stachys
10-31.text: O Holy Apostle Apostles Stachys, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

11-01.title: Troparion of Sts. Cosmas and Damian, Unmercenaries of Asia
11-01.text: O holy Unmercenaries and wonderworkers Cosmas and Damian, visit our infirmities; freely you have received, freely give to us.

11-02.title: Troparion of Martyrs Acindynus
11-02.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Acindynus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-03.title: Troparion of Martyrs Acepsimas the Bishop
11-03.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Acepsimas the Bishop, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-04.title: Troparion of St. Joannicius the Great of Bithynia
11-04.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Joannicius the Great of Bithynia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

11-05.title: Troparion of Martyrs Galaction and Episteme
11-05.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Galaction and Episteme, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-06.title: Troparion of St. Paul the Confessor
11-06.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Paul the Confessor, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

11-07.title: Troparion of Martyr Hieron of Melitene and thirty-three companions
11-07.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Hieron of Melitene and thirty-three companions, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-08.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
11-08.text: O Captains of the heavenly armies, we who are unworthy ever beseech you to deliver us by your supplications from terrors and to cover us with the protection of the wings of your immaterial glory, even as we fall down and cry to you: deliver us from danger, O captains of the angelic hosts.

11-09.title: Troparion of Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius; St. Matrona of Constantinople
11-09.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius; St. Matrona of Constantinople, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-10.title: Troparion of Apostles Erastus
11-10.text: O Holy Apostle Apostles Erastus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

11-11.title: Troparion of Great-martyr Menas of Egypt
11-11.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr Menas of Egypt, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-12.title: Troparion of St. John the Merciful
11-12.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. John the Merciful, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

11-13.title: Troparion of St. John Chrysostom
11-13.text: Grace shining forth from thy lips like a beacon hath enlightened the universe, and disclosed to the world the riches of poverty; it hath shown us the heights of humility. As thou dost instruct us by thy words, O John Chrysostom, our Father, so intercede with the Word, Christ our God, to save our souls.

11-14.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostle Philip
11-14.text: Holy Apostle Philip, intercede with the merciful God that He grant unto our souls forgiveness of sins.

11-15.title: Troparion of Beginning of the Nativity Fast; Martyrs and Confessors Gurias
11-15.text: Thy Martyr Beginning of the Nativity Fast; Martyrs and Confessors Gurias, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-16.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist Matthew
11-16.text: With zealous following thou didst hearken to Christ the Master who called thee, and having spurned the income of the custom-house thou didst show forth as a most-praised apostle and evangelist. Therefore, all-praised Matthew, entreat Christ God to grant remission of sins to those who honor thy memory.

11-17.title: Troparion of Gregory the Wonderworker
11-17.text: Thou didst become worthy of thy name through thy manner of life; through thy faith, O wonderworker Gregory, the city of Neocaesarea is well established. Wherefore, having boldness before Christ God, entreat Him to save our souls.

11-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Plato of Ancyra
11-18.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Plato of Ancyra, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-19.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Obadiah
11-19.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Obadiah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

11-20.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
11-20.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

11-21.title: Troparion of the Entrance of the Theotokos
11-21.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of mankind's salvation. The Virgin appeareth openly in the temple of God, and foretelleth Christ to all. Unto her, then, let us also cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's counsel.

11-21.title: Kontakion of the Entrance of the Theotokos
11-21.text: The most pure temple of the Saviour, the very precious bridal chamber and Virgin, the holy treasure of the glory of God, is led on this day into the house of the Lord, bringing with her the grace of the Divine Spirit. Of her doth the angels of God sing: 'Behold the heavenly tabernacle.'

11-22.title: Troparion of Apostles Philemon and Archippus
11-22.text: O Holy Apostle Apostles Philemon and Archippus, intercede with the merciful God that He may grant our souls the forgiveness of sins.

11-23.title: Troparion of Sts. Amphilochius of Iconium and Gregory of Acragas
11-23.text: O holy Sts. Amphilochius of Iconium and Gregory of Acragas, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

11-24.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Catherine
11-24.text: Let us praise the most-honorable bride of Christ, the divine Catherine, protectress of Sinai; for she silenced the eloquence of the impious by the power of the Spirit, and being crowned as a martyr, she now asks for all great mercy.

11-25.title: Troparion of Leavetaking of the Entrance; Hieromartyr Clement of Rome
11-25.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Leavetaking of the Entrance; Hieromartyr Clement of Rome, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

11-26.title: Troparion of St. Alypius the Stylite of Adrianopolis
11-26.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Alypius the Stylite of Adrianopolis, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

11-27.title: Troparion of Great-martyr James the Persian
11-27.text: Thy Great-martyr Great-martyr James the Persian, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-28.title: Troparion of Martyr Stephen the New of Mt. St. Auxentius
11-28.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Stephen the New of Mt. St. Auxentius, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-29.title: Troparion of Martyr Paramonus and three hundred and seventy with him
11-29.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Paramonus and three hundred and seventy with him, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

11-30.title: Troparion of the First-Called Apostle Andrew
11-30.text: O Apostle Andrew, first-called of all the Twelve and brother of him who was foremost of the chief Apostles, beseech the Master of all to grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

12-01.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Nahum
12-01.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Nahum, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-02.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Habakkuk
12-02.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Habakkuk, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-03.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Zephaniah
12-03.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Zephaniah, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-04.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Barbara
12-04.text: Let us honor the holy Barbara, for as a bird she escaped the snares of the enemy, and destroyed them through the help and defense of the Cross.

12-04.title: Troparion of St. John of Damascus
12-04.text: Orthodox image of the truth, you proved yourself to be a model of monastic life as well as of confession; as a true source of wisdom you sang inspired hymns. O wise and venerable John, pray to Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

12-05.title: Troparion of St. Sabbas the Sanctified
12-05.text: Thou didst prove to be a citizen of the desert, an angel in the flesh, and a wonderworker, O Sabbas, our God-bearing father. By fasting, vigil, and prayer thou didst obtain heavenly gifts, and thou healest the sick and the souls of those who turn to thee in faith. Glory to Him who gave thee strength; glory to Him who crowned thee; glory to Him who worketh healings for all through thee.

12-06.title: Troparion of St. Nicholas
12-06.text: The truth of things hath revealed thee to thy flock as a rule of faith, an icon of meekness, and a teacher of temperance; for which cause thou hast achieved the heights by humility, riches by poverty. O Father and Hierarch Nicholas, intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-06.title: Kontakion of St. Nicholas
12-06.text: Thou wast a true minister at Myra, O Saint Nicholas, and didst fulfill Christ's Gospel, laying down thy life for thy people and saving the innocent from death. Therefore thou hast been sanctified, as a great minister of the grace of God.

12-07.title: Troparion of St. Ambrose
12-07.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Ambrose, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

12-08.title: Troparion of St. Patapius of Thebes
12-08.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Patapius of Thebes, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-09.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
12-09.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

12-10.title: Troparion of Martyrs Menas
12-10.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Menas, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-11.title: Troparion of St. Daniel the Stylite
12-11.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Daniel the Stylite, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-12.title: Troparion of St. Spyridon the Wonderworker
12-12.text: In truth thou wast revealed to thy flock as a rule of faith, a model of meekness, and a teacher of temperance, so that by humility thou didst gain exaltation, and by poverty, riches. Holy Father St. Spyridon the Wonderworker, intercede with Christ our God that our souls may be saved.

12-13.title: Troparion of St. Herman of Alaska
12-13.text: Joyful North Star of the Church of Christ, guiding all people to the Heavenly Kingdom; teacher and apostle of the True Faith; intercessor and defender of the oppressed; adornment of the Orthodox Church in America: Blessed Father Herman of Alaska, pray to our Lord Jesus Christ to save our souls.

12-14.title: Troparion of Martyrs Thyrsus
12-14.text: Thy Martyr Martyrs Thyrsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-15.title: Troparion of Hieromartyr Eleutherius
12-15.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired Hieromartyr Eleutherius, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-16.title: Troparion of Holy Prophet Haggai
12-16.text: We celebrate the memory of thy prophet Holy Prophet Haggai, O Lord; through him we beseech Thee: save our souls.

12-17.title: Troparion of the Prophet Daniel and the Three Youths
12-17.text: Great are the achievements of faith! In the fountain of flame, as on the waters of rest, the three holy youths rejoiced. And the prophet Daniel was shown to be a shepherd of lions, as of sheep. Through their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

12-18.title: Troparion of Martyr Sebastian and his companions at Rome
12-18.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Sebastian and his companions at Rome, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-19.title: Troparion of Martyr Boniface of Tarsus
12-19.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Boniface of Tarsus, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-20.title: Troparion of the Hieromartyr Ignatius
12-20.text: Sharing the ways of the apostles and successor to their thrones, O God-inspired, thou hast found in active life the path to divine contemplation. Wherefore, ordering rightly the word of truth, thou didst contest for the faith even unto blood, O Hieromartyr Ignatius. Intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-20.title: Kontakion of the Hieromartyr Ignatius
12-20.text: From the East today hath the radiant star arisen, illumining the faithful with the splendor of his miracles. Wherefore let us crown with hymns Ignatius the God-bearer, the heavenly worshipper.

12-21.title: Troparion of St. Juliana of Nicomedia
12-21.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Juliana of Nicomedia, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.

12-22.title: Troparion of the Great-martyr Anastasia
12-22.text: Thy lamb Anastasia cries out to Thee, O Jesus, with a great voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I run to Thee. I am crucified with Thee and baptized into Thy death; I suffer for Thy sake that I may reign with Thee, and die for Thee that I may live in Thee. As a spotless sacrifice receive her who with love is sacrificed for Thee. By her prayers, as Thou art merciful, save our souls.

12-23.title: Troparion of Ten Martyrs of Crete
12-23.text: Thy Martyr Ten Martyrs of Crete, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-24.title: Troparion of Eve of the Nativity of Christ; Virgin-martyr Eugenia of Rome
12-24.text: Thy lamb Eve of the Nativity of Christ; Virgin-martyr Eugenia of Rome, O Jesus, crieth out with a loud voice: I love Thee, my Bridegroom, and seeking Thee I contest. I am crucified with Thee, and in Thy baptism am buried with Thee. I suffer for Thy sake, that I might reign with Thee; I die for Thee, that I might live in Thee. Receive me, then, an unblemished sacrifice that I have offered to Thee in love. By her prayers save our souls.

12-25.title: Troparion of the Nativity of Christ
12-25.text: Thy Nativity, O Christ our God, hath shone forth upon the world the light of knowledge; for thereby they that worshipped the stars were taught by a star to worship Thee, the Sun of Righteousness, and to know Thee, the Dayspring from on high. O Lord, glory to Thee.

12-25.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of Christ
12-25.text: Today the Virgin giveth birth to Him who is transcendent in essence, and the earth offereth a cave to Him who is unapproachable. Angels with shepherds give glory, and Magi journey with a star, for to us is born a young Child, the pre-eternal God.

12-26.title: Troparion of the Most Holy Theotokos
12-26.text: Today is the prelude of the good will of God, of the preaching of the salvation of mankind. The Virgin is exalted in the temple of God to make known Christ to all. To Her, then, with mighty voice let us cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's plan.

12-27.title: Troparion of the Protomartyr Stephen
12-27.text: O Protomartyr and servant of Christ, thou didst conquer in struggle and didst overthrow the wickedness of the enemies. As the first to be crowned, glorious Stephen, thou prayest now to Christ for the salvation of our souls.

12-28.title: Troparion of The 20
12-28.text: Thy Martyr The 20, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-29.title: Troparion of The 14
12-29.text: O holy The 14, pray to Christ our God that He save our souls.

12-30.title: Troparion of Martyr Anysia of Thessalonica
12-30.text: Thy Martyr Martyr Anysia of Thessalonica, O Lord, through his sufferings hath received the incorruptible crown from Thee, our God; for, having Thy strength, he cast down the tyrants and broke the feeble audacity of the demons. Through his intercessions save our souls.

12-31.title: Troparion of St. Melania the Younger of Rome
12-31.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. By the radiance of miracles thou didst illumine the whole universe. O St. Melania the Younger of Rome, our holy father, pray to Christ our God to save our souls.
